name: cdk8s (CDK for Kubernetes)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); AWS / CNCF sandbox project

performance.notes: Synthesizes to YAML at build time; no runtime overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Define Kubernetes manifests in TypeScript/Python/Java/Go. Synthesizes to YAML for kubectl apply. Languages: TypeScript (best), Python, Java, Go. cdk8s-plus: higher-level abstractions (Deployment, Service, Ingress, etc.) with sane defaults. cdk8s import to generate types from CRDs. Chart, App, and Construct abstractions. Works with any Kubernetes cluster. Helm charts can be imported. Better than raw YAML: type safety, loops, conditions. Helm is alternative with wider adoption and chart ecosystem.

gotchas: Less adoption than Helm for Kubernetes packaging
Python/Java support less polished than TypeScript
Generated YAML needs review — not always human-friendly
cdk8s-plus is opinionated — not all K8s features exposed
best_when: TypeScript teams managing Kubernetes manifests programmatically
Complex K8s deployments needing loops, conditionals, abstractions
Organizations with custom CRDs needing typed bindings
AWS CDK teams extending to Kubernetes with familiar patterns
avoid_when: Simple K8s deployments (kubectl + YAML or Helm sufficient)
Teams distributed across language preferences (Helm universal)
Need package/distribute K8s apps (Helm charts better ecosystem)
summary: cdk8s defines Kubernetes manifests in TypeScript/Python/Java/Go, synthesizing to YAML for kubectl apply. cdk8s-plus provides higher-level abstractions with sane defaults. Type safety, loops, and conditionals over raw YAML. CRD import for typed custom resources. CNCF sandbox project by AWS. Best for TypeScript teams managing complex K8s deployments programmatically. Helm has broader adoption for packaging/distributing K8s applications.
